title: ADF Launches Talisman Sabre 2025 Indo-Pacific Exercise with AUKUS Partners
summary: Australian Defence Force announces Talisman Sabre 2025, largest-ever multinational exercise. AUKUS allies join 35 nations in Indo-Pacific training. Focus targets amphibious operations, cyber defense, long-range strikes.
contents:
## ADF Launches Talisman Sabre 2025 Indo-Pacific Exercise with AUKUS Partners

### Overview
Australian Defence Force announces Talisman Sabre 2025, largest-ever multinational exercise. AUKUS allies join 35 nations in Indo-Pacific training. Focus targets amphibious operations, cyber defense, long-range strikes.

### Event Details
Australian Department of Defence reveals Talisman Sabre 2025 details through @DefenceAust posts. Exercise commences July 2025 across Queensland and northern Australia, involving over 30,000 personnel from 35 countries. United States, United Kingdom, Japan, and other AUKUS partners lead joint maneuvers. Troops conduct amphibious landings, urban combat simulations, and high-end warfare scenarios. Royal Australian Navy deploys ships including HMAS Adelaide for integrated task groups. Royal Australian Air Force flies F-35A Lightning II jets alongside US F-22 Raptors. ADF Army showcases M1A2 Abrams tanks and HIMARS rocket systems in live-fire drills. Exercise tests interoperability under AUKUS Pillar II technologies, including advanced AI command systems and hypersonic missile defenses. Commanders emphasize deterrence against Indo-Pacific threats. @DefenceAust shares videos of planning conferences and troop preparations. Officials highlight exercise scale surpasses previous iterations, with expanded maritime domains and space operations. Partners exchange tactics on contested logistics and undersea warfare. Indigenous communities participate in cultural awareness training. Exercise strengthens alliances amid rising regional tensions. Defence Minister praises ADF readiness demonstrations. Full schedule outlines 10 weeks of intense activities, culminating in multi-domain battle simulations. International observers monitor progress for future collaborations.

### Source Context
@DefenceAust (Australia Defence) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on australia department defence adf aukus indo-pacific exercise announcement.
